source: Egypt Ministry of Health and Population (MoHP) Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI); WHO EMRO Egypt country programme; UNICEF Egypt
source_notes: Schedule composed from WHO EMRO Egypt EPI programme description, UNICEF Egypt vaccines page, and the WHO Immunization Data portal for Egypt (EGY). Routine EPI coverage in Egypt exceeds 95%; WHO certified Egypt measles-elimination in 2023. Clinically reviewed by Ahmed Zayed MD. PCV, Rotavirus, Varicella, and Hepatitis A are NOT in the free public EPI — they are available privately (PAN-equivalent / paediatric society recommendation) and carry variable uptake.
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (EPI) and recommended (private) antigens with category distinction.
context_note: Destination-country schedule. In cross-border reconciliation INTO Egypt, incoming doses from a source country (e.g. Nigeria) are validated against this schedule — does each documented dose count under Egyptian EPI rules, what is missing, and what does the child need to enrol in an Egyptian nursery or school?
key_features: OPV is routine throughout Egyptian EPI (Egypt has been polio-free since 2006 but continues OPV)
Hexavalent at 2/4/6 months includes IPV, so IPV is covered via Hexavalent without a separate IPV dose
DPT uses DTPw (whole-cell pertussis) in public EPI; DTaP available privately
BCG at 1 month (within first month) — Egypt EPI convention, not WHO-birth-dose
Egypt transitioned measles-monovalent at 9m to MMR at 12m + 18m around 2020; pre-2020 cards may show measles-only at 9m
Yellow Fever is NOT routine in Egypt (non-endemic); arriving cards with YF should be preserved on record but not counted as missing
Rotavirus and PCV are recommended/private in Egypt; absence on an EPI card is expected, not a deficiency

doses.notes: Zero dose at birth (day 1 of life). Standalone HepB birth dose.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Egyptian EPI administers BCG at 1 month (within first 30 days) rather than at birth. WHO SAGE recommends at birth in TB-endemic countries; Egypt's MoHP convention places it at the first well-child visit at 1 month.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV zero dose at 1 month. Egypt uses bivalent OPV (bOPV) throughout its routine schedule.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Egypt's 6-in-1 vaccine covering DPT (whole-cell pertussis), Hib, Hepatitis B, and IPV. Given at 2 months. Egypt uses DTPw (whole-cell) in public EPI. Because IPV is bundled in Hexavalent, Egypt does not give a separate IPV dose (contrast with Nigeria, which gives Pentavalent 5-in-1 + separate IPV at 14 weeks).
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV dose 1 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 2 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV dose 2 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 4 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Third hexavalent dose at 6 months. Completes primary series.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV dose 3 of primary series, co-administered with Hexavalent at 6 months.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: MMR dose 1 at 12 months. Egypt transitioned from measles-monovalent to MMR; cards from children born before ~2020 may show measles-only at 9 months — that is a legacy record (see legacy_patterns).
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: OPV booster 1 at 12 months, co-administered with MMR dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: DPT booster (Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus) at 18 months. Standalone DPT booster — does not include Hib or HepB. May appear as 'Tresivac' or 'DPT' on Egyptian cards.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: MMR dose 2 at 18 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Diphtheria-Tetanus (DT) booster at school age (4–6 years). Does not include pertussis component.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended (not compulsory EPI). WHO-essential. PCV10 or PCV13. Variable uptake in public facilities; common in private practice.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt
Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended only — NOT compulsory EPI in Egypt. Available privately. Uptake is variable; typical Egyptian public-EPI cards will not show Varicella.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

doses.notes: Recommended second dose at 24 months. Minimum 4 weeks from dose 1.
doses.verification_source: Egypt MoHP EPI; WHO EMRO Egypt programme; UNICEF Egypt

legacy_patterns.description: Cards from children vaccinated before Egypt's MMR transition (~2020) may show different patterns. The agent should recognise these as valid historical records.

patterns.maps_to: Partial MMR coverage — Measles component only. Mumps and Rubella not covered.
patterns.action: Count as Measles dose 1; flag Mumps and Rubella as unverified.

patterns.maps_to: DTaP + Hib + HepB without IPV component
patterns.action: Count DTaP/Hib/HepB as given; flag IPV as not covered by that product.

interval_rules.notes: Egypt 2-4-6 schedule; standard 8-week interval. ACIP minimum 28 days.
